name: Composed California Corn Salad
recipeInstructions:
Note: In lieu of parboiling, the vegetables can be sautéed in olive oil until tender-crisp, if desired.
- Cut corn kernels from cobs into a bowl. Set aside.
- Bring 2 quarts salted water to a rolling boil. Drop bell pepper strips into boiling water. Parboil for 1 minute or until color intensifies. Remove peppers with a slotted spoon and drain. Refresh in a bowl of cold water. Set aside.
- Add yellow squash, zucchini, and eggplants to the boiling water. Parboil for 2 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain. Set aside.
- Toss basil with arugula and red leaf lettuce. Arrange greens on a large, shallow platter or tray.
- In a large bowl, stir together vinegar, walnut oil, and salt and pepper to taste. Add corn kernels to the dressing, tossing gently. Drain any excess dressing.
- Arrange corn kernels in a spoke pattern to resemble sun rays over the greens, mounding some in the center.
- Separately, add peppers, squash, zucchini, and eggplants to the dressing, tossing lightly. Arrange vegetables in sections between corn kernel spokes.
- Pour any remaining dressing over the salad. Makes 6 to 8 servings
